input.headline: How Troy Parrott broke the internet, in 5 easy memes

raw.headline: How Troy Parrott broke the internet, in 5 easy memes

winner: Troy Parrott broke the internet so hard, even his Wi-Fi password was "404 Error.

candidates.text: Troy Parrott didn't break the internet—he just broke the Wi-Fi password.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the headline with a specific tech reference, concise and funny."}

candidates.text: I’m not sure if he broke the internet or if he just finally found the one browser extension that actually fixes it.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the idea of breaking the internet, with a clever and specific reference to browser extensions."}

candidates.text: Troy Parrott broke the internet so hard, even his Wi-Fi password was "404 Error.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Funny, specific, and ties the headline to a humorous tech-related twist."}

candidates.text: Breaking the internet is like breaking a toaster—just plug it in and press 'start,' but make sure you unplug it first or you'll just get stuck with a screen that says 'Please wait while we connect... forever.'
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat relatable and has a mild twist, but it lacks specificity to Troy Parrott or the context of breaking the internet through memes. It's generic in its comparison and doesn't fully align with the headline's premise."}

candidates.text: Troy Parrott didn’t break the internet—he fixed a few buffering issues and got an upgrade for his router.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat humorous with a twist on the headline, but lacks surprise and specificity. It's generic in its approach and doesn't fully align with the meme format suggested by the headline."}
